type-systems.title: Dependent type - Wikipedia
type-systems.description: In intuitionistic type theory, dependent types are used to encode logic's quantifiers like 'for all' and 'there exists'. In functional programming languages like Agda, ATS, Coq, F*, Epigram, Idris, and Lean, dependent types help reduce bugs.

type-systems.title: Statically Typed vs Dynamically Typed Languages
type-systems.description: In statically typed programming languages, type checking occurs at compile time. At compile time, source code in a specific programming language is converted to a machine-readable format.

type-systems.title: Static vs. Dynamic Typing: Pros, Cons, and Key Differences
type-systems.description: Statically typed languages determine the type of a variable at compile time, whereas dynamically typed languages decide it at runtime. Static typing is a system for programming languages.

type-systems.description: Statically typed languages often outperform their dynamically typed language counterparts due to the optimizations made by the compiler.

type-systems.description: Modal types allow the type system to reason about scope in a very general sense. So potential applications are very broad and not thoroughly understood yet.
